summary: Ran backend pytest regression plus frontend Playwright on public preview URL for requested fixes. Product Changes panel and empty/live states now render, form-processing-results flow remains stable with longer timeout, and briefing schema/status checks pass. One backend schema gap remains: competitor-level source/has_live_data fields are computed but not exposed in API response model.

minor.issue: Competitor-level provenance fields (`source`, `has_live_data`) are dropped by response model even though orchestrator computes them; only top-level source_status is exposed.

action_items: If per-company provenance is required in UI/analytics, add `source` and `has_live_data` to CompetitorIntelligence response model.
Keep monitoring live upstream channel quality (news/product density) across more competitor pairs.
critical_code_review_comments: `IntelligenceOrchestrator._empty_company_payload` and live payloads include provenance fields, but `models.CompetitorIntelligence` omits them, causing silent field stripping in FastAPI response serialization.
Frontend correctly uses `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL` and now includes required product change test ids and empty states.
context_for_next_testing_agent: This run validated requested fixes only: product changes panel presence/empty state, no synthetic empty-company strategic summary, processing-to-results path, and briefing schema/source status. Backend pytest now has 9 tests and passes fully.
rca of the issue: Root cause of remaining minor gap: response schema mismatch. Orchestrator returns `source` and `has_live_data` inside competitor payloads, but Pydantic `CompetitorIntelligence` excludes these keys, so FastAPI response_model filtering drops them.
